content.p: Use the Direct Comparison Test to determine if \renewcommand{\log}{\ln} \displaystyle \sum_{ n= {{k0}} }^\infty \frac{ {{top}} }{ {{bottom}} } converges.

outtro.p:
Note that \renewcommand{\log}{\ln} \displaystyle \frac{ {{top}} }{ {{bottom}} } is a positive, eventually decreasing sequence. We have that:
\renewcommand{\log}{\ln} 0 \leq \displaystyle \sum_{ n= {{k0}} }^\infty \frac{ {{top}} }{ {{bottom}} } \leq \sum_{ n= {{k0}} }^\infty \frac{ {{simptop}} }{ {{simpbottom}} } =\sum_{ n= {{k0}} }^\infty \frac{ {{simpcoeff}} }{ n^{{P}} } which converges by the p-test since {{P}} > 1. So by the direct comparison text, \renewcommand{\log}{\ln} \displaystyle \sum_{ n= {{k0}} }^\infty \frac{ {{top}} }{ {{bottom}} } converges.

content.p: Use the Direct Comparison Test to determine if \renewcommand{\log}{\ln} \displaystyle \sum_{ n= {{k0}} }^\infty \frac{ {{top}} }{ {{bottom}} } converges.

outtro.p:
Note that \renewcommand{\log}{\ln} \displaystyle \frac{ {{top}} }{ {{bottom}} } is a positive, eventually decreasing sequence. We have that:
\renewcommand{\log}{\ln} \displaystyle \sum_{ n= {{k0}} }^\infty \frac{ {{top}} }{ {{bottom}} } \geq \sum_{ n= {{k0}} }^\infty \frac{ {{simptop}} }{ {{simpbottom}} } =\sum_{ n= {{k0}} }^\infty \frac{ {{simpcoeff}} }{ n^{{P}} } which diverges by the p-test since {{P}} \leq 1 So by the direct comparison text, \renewcommand{\log}{\ln} \displaystyle \sum_{ n= {{k0}} }^\infty \frac{ {{top}} }{ {{bottom}} } diverges.
